data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. The express test is failing (returns `10.00` but expects `15.00`). Let me start `dotnet watch test` for you so tests re-run automatically on file changes.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET test project using MSTest. They want hot reload set up so they can iterate faster. The way to do this with dotnet is `dotnet watch test`, which will re-run tests whenever files change.

Let me first run the tests to see the current state, then start `dotnet watch test`.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-6d4e8a0d85e141e6bb1a056c9bbd3a0c && dotnet watch test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

`dotnet watch test` is running. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expected `15.00`, actual `10.00`

The watcher is active. Whenever you save a change to `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will automatically rebuild and re-run. The fix needed is on **line 24** — change `10.00m` to `15.00m` for the `"express"` case. Want me to fix it?
